judge_verdicts.reason: Justification cites POS scan data (13,600 units, per-SKU splits, average prices) but no such scan evidence is in the cited or provided sources, so those numeric claims cannot be reconciled with the evidence shown.
Justification names the promotion ID, contract sections, cites exact scan units per SKU, confirms TPR prices, and shows the calculation matching the deduction under cap—leaving no obvious opening.
The justification cites POS scan data (13,600 units, per-SKU breakdowns, average prices) as confirming the claim, but no such scan data is in the cited evidence — these figures are unsupported.

The deduction record (NC-105117) itself is not provided in the evidence, so the claimed mechanic, SKU, period, rate, 42-store count, tear sheets, and $4,200 amount cannot be verified against source; only the promo/contract terms are shown.

Justification asserts a signed display agreement and circular tear sheets exist, but the cited source evidence contains no proof-of-performance attachments—only the promo terms and contract sections—so the key fact the dispute turns on is unsupported.

The justification asserts specific proof-of-performance attachments (signed display agreement DA-2026-Q1-002 covering all 42 stores, circular tear sheets for weeks of 11/09 and 11/23) and deduction details (ref NC-105117, 42 stores billed, period, no prior settlement), none of which appear in the cited evidence — these are unsupported/invented claims.

Arithmetic error: 2,480 + 1,820 = 4,300 is correct, but the justification cites Meridian POS data and the 90-day/no-duplication facts that are not present in the provided evidence; however the core claim units×rate=4300×0.50=2150 reconciles — the failure is the unit subtotals sum correctly yet the POS figures are not in cited evidence, making the numeric claims unverifiable/inconsistent with shown evidence.
Names the governing promo, SKUs, rate, period match, POS-confirmed units, cap, timing per §3.3, and duplication check — leaving no obvious opening.
The justification cites Meridian POS scan data (4,300 units, per-SKU breakdown), a duplication check on hc-20447/B, and a 2025-11-14 submission date, none of which appear in the cited evidence.
Regional-phase rate $9,500 matches deduction, 812 stores within 900-store cap, date within window, amount within $51,500 funding cap and $10,000 threshold — all claims reconcile with cited evidence.

Names the governing promo and contract sections, matches the $9,500 regional rate, confirms 812 stores within the 900-store cap, verifies the promo window and non-duplication—leaving no obvious opening.

The justification claims an 'attached new-item setup confirmation documents 812 stores effective 2025-10-13' and a deduction date of 2025-10-23, but no such setup-confirmation document or deduction record appears in the cited evidence.

Justification asserts facts (promotion search returned zero results, attachment characterized as internal ad allocation with no Meridian ID) not supported by the only cited evidence (§3.1 and §3.2), so those claims cannot be reconciled with the evidence shown.
Cites §3.1/§3.2 verbatim, names the deduction ref VM-90105/SKU/period, and plainly states no authorized Meridian promotion exists, leaving no obvious opening.

The justification asserts a promotion search returned zero results and characterizes the attachment as an internal ad allocation summary, but only contract sections 3.1/3.2 are cited—no evidence for the search or attachment is shown.

The justification cites POS unit figures (11/03: 3,050; 11/10: 2,800; 11/17: 2,650; in-window 11,180) and average price $3.49 that are not present in any cited evidence, so those numeric claims cannot be reconciled; also the $0.60 rate and window dates are consistent, but the unsupported POS/price data breaks logical consistency.

The secondary $2.99 price argument cites a performance requirement of 'advertised price' $2.99 or lower, but the justification uses average unit price ($3.49) as if equivalent — a mismatch a retailer could contest; also the POS in-window figures aren't in cited evidence.

POS per-week figures (3,050/2,800/2,650), the $3.49 average price, and in-window 11,180 units are not present in any cited evidence, and Meridian's 'scan-claims-cap-and-window convention' is not shown.

Ground 2 claims a search of all active promotions returned zero results for MF-BV-103, but no such search evidence is cited — only PROMO-2026-Q1-007 is provided, which cannot substantiate an absence-across-all-promotions claim; also POS scan data referenced in ground 1 is not among cited evidence.
Cites PROMO-2026-Q1-007's exclusive SKU list (MF-SN-001/003) and contract §3.1 authorization requirement, plainly showing MF-BV-103 is uncovered, leaving no obvious opening.
Claims 'POS data contains zero scans for MF-BV-103' and 'settlement history confirms this is not a duplicate,' but no POS data or settlement history is in evidence.

All claims reconcile: prior settlement paid MF-BV-102 slotting $12,000 in full, §4.2 bars duplicate slotting absent new authorization, and §3.3 permits offset—arithmetic and logic are consistent with cited evidence.
Names prior settlement SH-2025-Q4-011, matching invoice/promotion/SKU, and contract §4.2's one-time slotting rule; clearly explains the duplicate basis and offset right, leaving no obvious opening.
The claim that 'a promotion search returned zero results' for new slotting is not supported by any cited evidence; no such search result is in evidence.
Rate $0.65, cap $12,000, SKU, and window all match cited evidence; 8,492×$0.65=$5,519.80 and 13,692×$0.65=$8,899.80 arithmetic reconciles, and partial payment on scanned units follows from §2.1.

Justification names the governing promo and contract §2.1, plainly explains the shipped-vs-scanned unit discrepancy with week-by-week POS figures, ties the reconciliation to the authorized rate, and confirms performance requirement — leaving no obvious opening.

The justification cites POS scan data (8,492 units, weekly breakdown), a claim worksheet with a 'shipped+scanned' column, average unit prices, and a duplicate check — none of which appear in the cited evidence (only the promo record and contract §2.1 are provided).

The performance requirement (feature in at least two weekly circulars) is never addressed, and POS/scan volume of 14,020 units is claimed but no such POS evidence is among the cited sources, so the 14,020-unit and cap arithmetic cannot be reconciled to shown evidence.
The promo requires featuring in at least two weekly circulars, but the justification never addresses whether this performance requirement was met, leaving an obvious opening for dispute.

The justification cites POS scanner data (14,020 units) and 'Meridian's established scan-claims-cap-and-window convention,' neither of which appears in the provided evidence; the promo also has a two-circular performance requirement not addressed.

The eligible-units figures (1,030 + 1,015 = 2,045) and the ad-proof/POS scan data are cited but not present in the provided evidence, so the unit eligibility arithmetic cannot be verified; rate and cap checks are consistent but the core numeric claim is unsupported by shown evidence.

The justification relies on ValuMax's ad proof and POS scan data establishing only two eligible feature weeks (2,045 units), but neither the ad proof nor the POS data is in the cited source evidence, leaving the ineligible-units determination unsupported and open to dispute.
The POS scan data (1,030+1,015 units) and the ad proof establishing only two feature weeks are cited as fact but appear in neither cited evidence item, making key figures unsupported.
Arithmetic (8,545 × $0.55 = $4,699.75) reconciles, rate/SKU/window/cap claims match the promotion evidence, and §2.2 supports the scan-data requirement justifying escalation.
Cites PROMO-2026-Q1-009 and §2.2 scan-data obligation, plainly states the missing weekly scan proof as the specific reason for hold, leaving no obvious opening.

Justification asserts specific facts (deduction ID HC-20712, claimed amount $4,699.75, 8,545 units, POS found=false, retailer one-line attachment with scan detail 'to follow', $10,000 auto-settlement threshold, non-duplicate status) that are not supported by any cited evidence provided.

Denial follows logically: §5.2's activity list excludes digital placements, §3.1 requires written pre-authorization with promotion ID (none cited), §5.3 requires review before deduction, and §3.3 permits offset—all claims reconcile with cited evidence; no arithmetic to verify since amount is denied in full.
Cites §5.2 exhaustive activity list excluding digital placements and §3.1/§5.3 authorization gaps, stating the key facts the dispute turns on with specific, professional reasoning.

The justification asserts a search of the authorized promotion calendar 'returned no MDF authorization' and references a 'campaign-report attachment' and remittance text, none of which appear in the cited evidence.

Claim (4) states $42,000 is within a $51,500 funding cap, but this ignores that regional phase ($9,500) plus chainwide ($42,000) would total $51,500; more critically, the justification asserts the invoicing condition in 4.2 is satisfied, yet 4.2 only requires the phase be confirmed and deducted once—consistent—however the funding-cap reasoning is sound; the actual inconsistency: evidence supports chainwide $42,000 but justification does not reconcile whether regional phase was already invoiced, though on the cited numbers all claims (rate, window, cap, non-duplicate) do reconcile with the evidence shown.

Justification cites the specific promo ID, contract sections 4.1/4.2, the chainwide rate, distribution confirmation exceeding the 900-store threshold, funding cap, and non-duplication — leaving no obvious opening for dispute.

Claim (3) asserts NorthCart attached a chainwide distribution confirmation showing 1,240 stores effective 2025-12-08, but no such document is in the cited evidence; also the deduction date claim isn't supported by any evidence shown.

Arithmetic reconciles ($250×44=$11,000, within $15,000 cap; 60%×$11,000=$6,600, remainder $4,400); precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 confirms 60% convention (5550/9250=60%), and all claims align with cited evidence.

Names the promotion, contract §3.4 requirement, cites the missing Exhibit B, quotes 'POP forms coming', details corroborating evidence, and applies precedent SH-2025-Q4-007's 60% convention with explicit math — leaving no obvious opening.

Specific figures (44 events, $11,000, 23 stores, Palmer email, 10,540 units scanned, 'POP forms coming') are cited to no evidence — the remittance, photos, email, and POS data are not among the provided evidence.

Arithmetic ($6,400 × 60% = $3,840), cap ($15,000), and rate comparison ($200 < $250) all reconcile with cited evidence; precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 settled at 60% consistently, and each factual claim follows from the shown sources.
Cites governing promo, contract §3.4 POP requirement, precedent for 60% haircut, and plainly explains the missing Exhibit B and corroborating evidence, leaving no obvious opening.
The POS scan figures (10,540 units, 6 weekly periods) and store photo details (6 stores/locations) are not present in any cited evidence, making them unsupported invented claims.

POS corroboration claims scan data throughout Oct 19–Nov 9, but cited scan promo PROMO-2026-Q1-007 runs 2025-10-13 to 2025-11-09; more critically, no actual POS unit/week figures (1,100–1,110) appear anywhere in the cited evidence, so the corroboration arithmetic is unsupported/inconsistent with what is shown.
Names the promotion, the specific contract clause (§3.4 Exhibit B requirement), the precedent basis for 60%, and cites both corroborators plus a reconsideration path — leaving no obvious opening.

Scan POS lift figures (1,100–1,110 units/week for the SKUs across Oct 19–Nov 9) and the '19 stores' photo count are cited as evidence but no such POS/scan data or photo attachment appears in the provided evidence.

POS scan week-by-week figures 980+1640+1720+1010=5350 reconcile, but 35 events × $300 = $10,500 and 60% = $6,300 are arithmetically correct; however the precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 settled at 5550/9250 = 60% consistently — all numbers reconcile, so actually consistent.

Cites governing promo, contract §3.4 requiring Exhibit B, states the missing-form reason plainly, and anchors the 60% haircut to documented precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 with corroborating photos and POS scan data — leaving no obvious opening.

The store photos (22 stores) and POS scan data (5,350 units, week-by-week figures) are asserted in the justification but appear in none of the cited evidence, making them unsupported invented figures.
All claims reconcile with evidence: rate $0.50/unit, both SKUs, period within window, 13,600×$0.50=$6,800 below $10,000 cap, and contract sections support the mechanic and documentation requirements.
Justification cites the governing promotion and contract sections, plainly reconciles rate, SKUs, period, unit count, math, cap, and no-duplicate against the evidence, leaving no obvious opening.

The justification claims POS/scan data confirms 13,600 units scanned and shelf prices held at TPR levels, but no POS data or scan summary is present in the cited evidence — these are unsupported assertions, along with the claim/deduction details never shown.

Performance requirement mandates display for minimum 3 weeks, but only two circular tear sheets (weeks ending 11/09 and 11/23) are cited, which does not clearly establish the required 3-week performance.

Performance requirement is a minimum 3-week endcap display, but the justification only cites tear sheets for weeks ending 11/09 and 11/23 without establishing the required 3-week duration—leaving the key proof-of-performance fact the dispute would turn on unaddressed.

The justification claims proof of performance (signed display agreement DA-2026-Q1-002 and circular tear sheets) was provided, plus a duplicate-check finding for NC-105117 and a $10,000 threshold, none of which appear in the cited evidence.

Unit breakdown 2,480 + 1,820 = 4,300 is correct, but the POS scan data and claim period are not in the cited evidence; however the arithmetic that IS shown (4,300 × $0.50 = $2,150, cap $9,000, 90-day window) reconciles — the failure is that the two-SKU subtotals actually sum to 4,300 correctly, so numbers reconcile internally.
Names the governing promotion and contract term, states POS-corroborated unit counts, calculation, funding cap, timing window, and duplicate check — leaving no obvious opening.

The justification cites POS scan data (4,300 units split as 2,480/1,820 across four weekly periods), duplicate-check results, submission date, and auto-settlement threshold, none of which appear in the cited evidence — these are unsupported/invented claims.
All claims reconcile with evidence: $9,500 matches regional-phase rate, 812 stores within 900 cap, date within window, phase deducted once, and amount below $10,000 threshold.

Justification names the promo and contract sections, cites the $9,500 regional-phase rate, confirms 812 stores within the 900-store cap, verifies date within window, and addresses duplicate/documentation checks—all supported by evidence.

The justification asserts a deduction date of 2025-10-23, a NorthCart setup confirmation documenting 812 stores effective 2025-10-13, a deduction reference NC-104550, and settlement history showing no prior settlement — none of which appear in the cited evidence.

Claims of a promotion search returning zero results and details about the deduction ref, amount, SKU, and attachment contents are not supported by the cited evidence, which contains only contract sections 3.1 and 3.2.
Cites §3.1 authorization requirement and §3.2 documentation standard, states no authorized promotion/promo ID exists, and specifies remedy path—leaving no obvious opening.

Claims about the promotion search returning zero results, the deduction ref VM-90105, the $7,300 amount, the SKU/period, and the ValuMax internal ad charge attachment are not supported by the only two cited evidence items (contract §3.1 and §3.2).

Promo period ends 2025-10-27, so the claimed weeks (11/03–11/17) are outside the promotional window per §2.3, contradicting the justification's in-period claim; also funding cap is $8,000 and the promo is named 'October' Scandown, not covering November.

The claimed weeks (11/03–11/17) fall entirely outside the promo's stated period (ends 2025-10-27), violating §2.3, and exceed the $8,000 funding cap—the justification's core validity claims contradict the cited evidence, leaving obvious openings for dispute.

Claims the promo end date is 10/27 yet approves 11/03–11/17 scans as in-window; the evidence shows the promo period ended 2025-10-27, so the POS 'in-period' reconciliation and window compliance are unsupported/contradicted, and the $8,000 funding cap is ignored.

Justification claims POS scan data for PROMO-2026-Q1-007 was retrieved and a full promotion search was run, but no such POS data or search-result evidence is cited—only the promo record showing it covers MF-SN-001/003, so the zero-scan and zero-authorization claims are not reconciled with the evidence shown.
Names the cited promo and its actual SKU coverage, invokes contract §3.1/§2.1, and plainly states MF-BV-103 is neither authorized nor scanned—leaving no obvious opening.

The justification claims POS scan data was retrieved and a full promotion search was run returning zero results, but no such POS data or promotion-search evidence is in the cited sources — those are uncited assertions.

All claims reconcile: prior settlement SH-2025-Q4-011 paid $12,000 for VM-88214/SLOT-VM-2025-09/MF-BV-102, §4.2 supports one-time slotting rule, and §3.3 supports offset/recovery right; denial follows logically.

Names duplicate prior settlement SH-2025-Q4-011 with matching invoice/deal/SKU/amount, cites §4.2 one-time slotting rule and §3.3 offset right, and notes no new authorization submitted—leaving no obvious opening.

Justification claims invoice VM-88214 and matching dollar amount as identical, but prior settlement shows deduction_ref VM-88214 and current claim VM-90332 differ; also asserts §3.3 permits 'recover the deducted amount' whereas §3.3 only allows offset after dispute concludes, and the claimed 'exact/identical invoice number' contradicts differing references.
Arithmetic reconciles: 8,492 units x $0.65 = $5,519.80, below $12,000 cap; scan-only payment aligns with §2.1, and rate/window/cap match the promo evidence.

Names the promo and contract terms, states scan-only payment rule, cites verified weekly POS scans vs. claimed units, and explains the overstatement with worksheet 'shipped+scanned' notation—leaving no obvious opening.

The justification asserts specific weekly scan figures (2,140/2,210/2,080/2,062 = 8,492 units), avg unit price $7.99 per week, claimed 13,692 units/$8,899.80, a 'shipped+scanned' worksheet column, 90-day submission, and no-duplicate status—none of which appear in the cited evidence, which contains only the promo terms and contract text.
Arithmetic reconciles: 14,020 × $0.70 = $9,814, capped at $7,500 per funding cap; all claims consistent with cited promotion and contract §2.1.
Cites promo ID, contract §2.1 cap language, reconciles scan data, and applies min(units×rate, cap)=$7,500 with clear reasoning leaving no obvious opening.
POS scan data (14,020 units), deduction reference VM-90455, no-prior-settlement claim, and Meridian's scan-cap convention are asserted but none appear in the cited evidence.
All numbers reconcile: authorized rate $1.00 × 4,000 units = $4,000 valid, excess $0.50 × 4,000 = $2,000 denied, within $6,000 cap; consistent with cited evidence.
Names the promotion and contract §2.2, states authorized $1.00 rate vs deducted $1.50, corroborates units with POS data, and cleanly justifies denying the $2,000 excess with no obvious opening.
The POS scan data (4,215 units) and ValuMax's submitted ad proof for weeks 10/26 and 11/09 are cited as established facts but appear in no provided evidence.
Claims reconcile: $0.55 rate and window match promo, 8,545 x $0.55 = $4,699.75 below $6,500 cap, timing within 90 days, and scan-data absence justifies escalation per §2.2.

Names the specific promo, contract sections 2.2/3.3, and plainly states the missing POS scan data as the reason for escalation, leaving no obvious opening since scan-based payment requires verified units.
Claims $4,699.75 equals 8,545 units at $0.55 and cites POS get_pos_data found=false and a retailer attachment, none of which appear in the cited evidence.

Each ground follows from cited contract sections; §5.2 excludes digital placements, §5.3 requires pre-review, §3.1 requires promotion ID, and no cited promotion authorizes MDF digital activity, so denial reconciles with evidence.

Names specific contract sections (5.2 exhaustive qualifying activities, 5.3 pre-approval, 3.1 promo ID), explains digital placements are non-qualifying, and confirms no matching promotion exists — leaving no obvious opening.

The justification claims 'The MDF contract section exhaustively defines' and 'use of are (not include) signals an exhaustive list' — the contract does not itself state exhaustiveness; this is an unsupported interpretive assertion. Also claims '5.3 requires pre-deduction approval' but §5.3 only says claims are 'subject to Meridian review,' and asserts 'No pre-authorization is on file' without any evidence of a checked file.

Justification claims chainwide phase was confirmed, but §4.2 allows each phase once per item; regional phase (up to 900 stores) plus chainwide would exceed cap—yet the confirmation cites 1,240 stores with no evidence distinguishing regional vs chainwide, and no source evidence supports the distribution confirmation of 1,240 stores at all.
Cites promo ID, contract §4.1/§4.2 fee terms, distribution confirmation for 1,240 stores, promo window, and non-duplication—specific and complete, leaving no obvious opening.

The claim that NorthCart submitted a chainwide distribution confirmation for 1,240 stores effective 2025-12-08 is not supported by any cited evidence; no distribution confirmation document appears in the source.

All numbers reconcile: 44×$250=$11,000 within $15,000 cap, 60%×$11,000=$6,600; precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 shows 60% (5550/9250) demo billback convention, and §3.4 evidence supports non-payability without Exhibit B — all claims consistent with cited evidence.

Cites the governing promo and §3.4 Exhibit B requirement, plainly states the missing proof-of-performance as the reason, and grounds the 60% partial in documented precedent SH-2025-Q4-007, leaving no obvious opening.
The claimed evidence of store photos from 23 stores, POS scan lift of 10,540 units, and J. Palmer's email are not present in the cited source evidence, making these corroboration claims unsupported.

Justification says $200/event is 'used as the operative rate,' yet 32 events × $200 = $6,400 and the settlement applies 60% to that claimed amount; but if the operative rate were truly applied, the corroborated 6 stores don't reconcile — more critically, the claim total $6,400 implies $200/event while promo authorizes $250, and the 60%×$6,400=$3,840 arithmetic is internally correct, but the rate reasoning contradicts itself by both citing $200 as operative and settling on claimed dollars.
Names the promo, the §3.4 Exhibit B requirement, the documentation gap, corroborating evidence, precedent, and the window overage—leaving no obvious opening for a counterparty.

The POS scan data claim (consistent week-over-week lift for both SKUs across six weeks, 10,540 total units) and the '6 stores' photo detail are cited as corroboration but no such POS/photo evidence appears in the provided source evidence.
All numbers reconcile: 30×$250=$7,500 within $12,000 cap; 60% precedent (5550/9250=60%) yields $4,500; remaining $3,000 denied; below $10k threshold — all consistent with cited evidence.

Names the governing promo and contract sections, states the Exhibit B documentation gap plainly, cites corroborating photos/scan-lift and the 60% precedent (SH-2025-Q4-007) consistent with the source, and offers a re-submission path — leaving no obvious opening.

The store photos (19 stores) and POS scan-lift figures (MF-SN-001 peaks at 1,240 units week ending 2025-10-25 vs 690-720) are cited as evidence but appear in no provided source; these are unsupported invented figures.
Numbers reconcile: 35×$300=$10,500 within $18,000 cap; 60%×$10,500=$6,300; precedent SH-2025-Q4-007 shows 5550/9250=60%, all consistent with cited evidence.

The justification cites store photos from 22 stores and specific POS scan data (5,350 units with weekly breakdown) plus the '35 events' count and 'no duplicate settlement' claim, none of which appear in the cited evidence.
